title: Entry Visa for Residents of GCC Countries and Their Companions

fees: Application fee: AED 250; Vat: 5%
processing_time: 48 hours | Validity: 30 days | Extension: Can be extended once for an additional 30 days | Sponsor required: No

documents: Valid Emirates ID; Valid eye test report; Existing driving licence (if applicable)
steps: Log in using Emirates ID, Passport, or UAE Pass; Select a driving institute; Submit the application; Pay initial fees; Receive learning permit; Complete driving training; Pass eye test; Pass knowledge, yard, and road tests; Pay licence issuance fees; Receive digital or physical driving licence

processing_time: Instant after passing tests and paying fees | Target audience: UAE citizens; UAE residents | Available channels: RTA Website; RTA Smart App; Driving Institutes

documents: Valid Emirates ID; Valid eye test report; Medical fitness report (for professional drivers)

fees: Licence under 21: AED 100; Licence 21 or above: AED 300; Eye test: AED 140 - AED 180; Knowledge and innovation fee: AED 20; Late renewal fine: AED 10 per month (maximum AED 500)

processing_time: Instant (around 1 minute after approval) | Target audience: UAE residents with valid foreign driving licence | Available channels: Website; Mobile App; Service Centers

steps: Select licence type (Normal / Instant / eTrader); Submit application online or via service centre; Provide required documents; Obtain approvals if needed; Pay licence fees; Receive trade licence digitally
fees: Varies by activity: True; Etrader fixed cost: AED 1070 + AED 300 | Licence type options: Type: Normal Licence; Description: Standard business licence requiring Memorandum of Association and physical office lease.; Requirements: Memorandum of Association (MoA); Site lease contract; External approvals if required; Type: Instant Licence; Description: Fast-track licence issued within minutes for selected activities without external approvals.; Includes: Trade licence; Dubai Chamber membership; Establishment card (GDRFA); Establishment card (MOHRE); Option to employ up to 3 employees; Eligible legal forms: LLC; LLC Single Owner; Sole Establishment; Civil Company; Type: eTrader Licence; Description: Licence for home-based businesses and sole establishments in Dubai.; Requirements: Valid Emirates ID; Registered trade name; Fees: Total cost: AED 1070 + AED 300 (Dubai Chamber membership)
processing_time: Normal licence: Up to 10 minutes via service centres (varies by approvals); Instant licence: Around 5 minutes; Etrader licence: Around 5 minutes | Available channels: Invest in Dubai Portal; Service Centres

title: Visit Visa for Visiting Relatives and Friends
eligibility: Passport must be valid for at least 6 months; Valid health insurance in UAE is required; Sponsor must meet minimum salary requirement depending on relationship; No entry restrictions to UAE; Proof of relationship must be provided for relatives | Salary requirements: First degree relatives: AED 4,000 minimum monthly salary; Second and third degree relatives: AED 8,000 minimum monthly salary; Friends: AED 15,000 minimum monthly salary | Relationship categories: First degree: Father, Mother, Spouse, Son, Daughter; Second degree: Siblings, Grandparents, Grandchildren; Third degree: Uncle, Aunt, Cousins; Friend: Non-relative | Important notes: Health insurance is mandatory in UAE; Passport must be valid for at least 6 months; Overstay fine applies from the day after visa expiry; Visa extension depends on UAE authority approval; Proof of kinship is strictly required for relatives; Salary requirements differ based on relationship category
documents: Passport (valid for at least 6 months); Personal passport-size photo; Proof of kinship (for relatives); Residence permit copy of sponsor (if applicable); Justification for visit; Medical report (if required); Copy of GCC residence permit (if applicable); Copy of identification document (for Afghanistan / Iran / Iraq cases); Return flight ticket
steps: Log in to UAE smart services portal or GDRFA system; Register or sign in using UAE Pass or username; Select 'Visit Visa for Relatives and Friends' service; Fill in sponsor and applicant details; Upload required documents; Verify relationship and eligibility criteria; Pay application fees and security deposit if required; Submit application for approval; Receive visa approval via email or system notification; Visa is issued and sent digitally
fees: Application fee: AED 100; Single entry visa per month: AED 100; Multiple entry visa per month: AED 200; Smart service fee: AED 100; Single entry financial guarantee: AED 1000; Multiple entry financial guarantee: AED 2000 | Fines: Overstay penalty per day: AED 50 per day | Refund policy: Financial guarantee may be refunded after visa cancellation or exit from UAE if no violations exist
processing_time: Not specified in source data | Validity: Tourist stay: Up to 90 days continuous stay; Maximum extension: Up to 180 days total within one year | Extension: Visa may be extended once depending on approval | Target audience: UAE residents sponsoring relatives; Residents sponsoring friends; Family members of UAE residents; Friends visiting UAE residents

title: Residence Visa Renewal
eligibility: Holder of an existing UAE residence visa (employment, family-sponsored, or property-based) approaching expiry, with an active sponsor (employer, family sponsor, or self if property owner). | Important notes: Renewal should ideally be initiated before expiry; a grace period applies after expiry but overstay fines accrue once it lapses.
documents: Passport copy valid 6+ months, current/expiring visa copy, Emirates ID copy, recent passport-size photo with white background, medical fitness certificate (typically required again for renewal), valid health insurance, sponsor's documents (trade license copy for employer sponsorship, or proof of relationship/tenancy/ownership for family or property sponsorship).
steps: 1) Complete a medical fitness test at an approved center. 2) Sponsor (employer, family member, or self) submits the renewal application via GDRFA/ICP smart services or a typing/Amer center. 3) Pay renewal fees. 4) Update or renew Emirates ID alongside the visa. 5) Receive renewed visa, typically valid for the same duration as the original (1, 2, 3, 5, or 10 years depending on visa type).
fees: Approximately AED 200-650 for the residence permit fee depending on visa type and duration, plus Emirates ID renewal fee (approximately AED 100-400 depending on validity period), medical test fee (approximately AED 250-350), and standard Knowledge and Innovation Dirham fees (approximately AED 20 total). Subject to change and varies significantly by visa category - always confirm current fees on the official portal.
processing_time: Typically same-day to a few business days once all documents and the medical test are complete, though processing can take longer if additional approvals are required. | Renewable: Yes, recurring on the same cycle as the original visa validity. | Important: Start the renewal process at least a few weeks before expiry to avoid the grace period or overstay fines.

title: Residence Visa Cancellation
eligibility: Any current residence visa holder whose sponsorship is ending - due to job loss, end of contract, family sponsor relationship ending, leaving the UAE permanently, or switching to a new sponsor/visa type. | Important notes: Cancellation must be initiated by the current sponsor (employer or family sponsor) in most cases, not the visa holder alone, except in specific self-sponsored cases.
documents: Passport copy, current visa copy, Emirates ID (to be cancelled/returned), labor card cancellation (for employment visas, handled via MOHRE), any outstanding fine clearance proof, exit confirmation if leaving the UAE.
steps: 1) Sponsor (employer or family sponsor) initiates cancellation request through GDRFA/ICP smart services, MOHRE (for employment cases), or a typing/Amer center. 2) Clear any outstanding fines or dues. 3) Cancel the associated labor card if applicable (employment visas). 4) Receive cancellation confirmation. 5) A grace period (commonly around 30 days, though it can vary by case and visa type) typically follows during which the person may stay to settle affairs, find a new sponsor, or exit the country before overstay fines apply.
fees: Cancellation itself is often low-cost or free depending on visa type and sponsor, but outstanding fines, unpaid fees, or labor-related dues must be cleared first. Approximately AED 0-200 in direct cancellation service fees in many cases; confirm exact figure for the specific visa type on the official portal.
processing_time: Often same-day to a few business days once submitted with complete documentation and no outstanding dues. | Important: After cancellation, a grace period applies before the person must exit the UAE or secure a new visa status - overstaying beyond this grace period results in daily overstay fines.

title: Visa Overstay - Fines and What To Do
eligibility: Applies to anyone whose visa (visit, tourist, residence, or any other UAE visa type) has expired or been cancelled and who remains in the UAE beyond the applicable grace period without renewing, transferring, or exiting.
documents: Passport, expired/cancelled visa copy, Emirates ID (if applicable), proof of any fine payments already made.
steps: 1) Confirm exact overstay status and accrued fine amount via the GDRFA or ICP smart services app/website, or at a Customer Happiness/Amer center. 2) Settle outstanding fines in full before any further visa action (renewal, status change, or exit) can be processed. 3) Either renew/regularize status under a valid visa, or arrange to exit the country. 4) For exit, ensure fines are cleared before departure to avoid travel bans or re-entry issues. 5) In genuine cases of inability to pay or special hardship, some amnesty periods have historically been announced by UAE authorities - check official channels for any active amnesty program.
fees: Overstay fines are commonly cited at approximately AED 50 per day past the grace period for most visa types (this figure varies by visa category and has changed in past regulations, so always confirm the current daily rate on the official portal). Fines accrue daily until settled.
processing_time: Fine settlement itself is typically immediate upon payment, but resolving the overall status (renewal, transfer, or exit clearance) can take additional time depending on the path chosen. | Important: Do not delay addressing an overstay - fines accrue daily and unresolved overstays can affect future UAE entry.

title: Green Visa - Self-Sponsored Residence
eligibility: Available to skilled employees, freelancers/self-employed individuals, and investors who meet category-specific criteria, without requiring a traditional employer or family sponsor. Common qualifying groups include: skilled employees with a valid employment contract and recognized qualification meeting a minimum salary threshold; freelancers/self-employed individuals holding a valid freelance permit and meeting income/qualification requirements; and investors with qualifying business investment. | Important notes: The Green Visa allows self-sponsorship, meaning the holder does not need an employer or family member to sponsor their residency, and it allows sponsorship of immediate family members by the holder.
documents: Passport copy valid 6+ months, recent passport-size photo, proof of qualifying category (employment contract and salary certificate for skilled employees; freelance permit and income proof for freelancers; investment proof for investors), educational qualification certificate (attested, where required), health insurance, Emirates ID application.
steps: 1) Confirm eligibility category (skilled employee, freelancer, or investor). 2) Obtain or confirm required supporting documents (freelance permit from the relevant free zone authority for freelancers, or employment/investment proof for other categories). 3) Submit application via ICP/GDRFA smart services or an authorized typing/Amer center. 4) Complete medical fitness test and Emirates ID biometrics. 5) Receive Green Visa, typically valid for 5 years and renewable.
fees: Total cost typically in the range of approximately AED 2,000-3,000+ depending on category and add-ons (residence permit fee, Emirates ID fee, Knowledge and Innovation Dirham fees, and any category-specific charges such as freelance permit costs). Subject to change and varies by free zone/category - confirm current fees on the official portal.
processing_time: Typically a few days to a few weeks depending on category and completeness of documentation. | Validity: 5 years, renewable, self-sponsored (no employer/family sponsor required). | Holders may sponsor their spouse and children under this visa.

title: Freelance Permit / License
eligibility: Open to individuals wishing to work as independent freelancers in approved activities (commonly media, technology, education/training, design, consulting, and similar knowledge-based fields), typically issued through specific free zones (e.g., media free zones, technology free zones) rather than mainland Department of Economy in most emirates. Eligibility and approved activity lists vary by issuing free zone authority.
documents: Passport copy, passport-size photo, CV or proof of relevant experience/qualifications in the chosen activity, portfolio or proof of work (for creative/media activities, where required), bank reference letter (in some cases), No Objection Certificate from current employer if already UAE-employed and applying as a side freelance permit.
steps: 1) Choose an approved freelance activity and the free zone authority that issues permits for it. 2) Submit application with required documents to the chosen free zone authority. 3) Pay permit fees. 4) Receive freelance permit, which can then be used to apply for a residence visa (including potentially a Green Visa) if the holder wishes to self-sponsor residency on this basis.
fees: Varies significantly by free zone and activity type, commonly ranging from approximately AED 7,500-15,000+ per year depending on the issuing authority and package selected (some packages include visa eligibility, others are permit-only). Subject to change - confirm current fees with the specific free zone authority.
processing_time: Typically a few days to about two weeks depending on the free zone authority and completeness of documentation.

title: Professional License (Service-Based Business)
eligibility: For individuals or entities offering professional/consultancy services (e.g., legal consultancy, engineering consultancy, medical practice, accounting, IT consultancy, and similar service-based professions) rather than trading in goods. Some professional activities require the practitioner to hold relevant professional qualifications and, in regulated fields (e.g., legal, medical, engineering), approval from the relevant regulatory authority in addition to the standard licensing process.
documents: Passport copies of all partners/owner, educational/professional qualification certificates (attested, relevant to the licensed activity), proof of relevant experience where required, trade name reservation certificate, initial approval certificate, tenancy contract (Ejari) for office space, regulatory authority approval for regulated professions (e.g., health authority approval for medical practices).
steps: 1) Choose the specific professional activity and confirm if it requires extra regulatory approval. 2) Reserve a trade name. 3) Obtain initial approval from the Department of Economy (or relevant free zone authority). 4) Obtain any required regulatory authority approval for the specific profession. 5) Secure office space and register the tenancy contract (Ejari). 6) Submit final application and pay license fees to receive the professional license.
fees: Varies by emirate, activity, and office space; commonly ranges from approximately AED 8,000-15,000+ depending on the profession and any additional regulatory approval fees. Subject to change - confirm current fees with the relevant Department of Economy or free zone authority.
processing_time: Typically 1-3 weeks depending on whether additional regulatory approvals are required for the specific profession.

title: Trade License Renewal
eligibility: Business owners holding an existing UAE trade license (mainland or free zone) approaching its annual expiry date, with no major outstanding violations or unresolved regulatory issues that would block renewal.
documents: Current trade license copy, valid tenancy contract/Ejari (renewed if applicable), passport and Emirates ID copies of owner(s)/partners, any required regulatory authority renewal approvals (for regulated activities), proof of any required insurance.
steps: 1) Ensure the office tenancy contract (Ejari) is renewed and valid, if applicable to the license type. 2) Settle any outstanding fines or dues with the Department of Economy or relevant authority. 3) Submit renewal application through the Department of Economy portal, free zone authority portal, or a registered business service center. 4) Pay renewal fees. 5) Receive renewed trade license, typically valid for one year.
fees: Varies by emirate, business activity, and license type; renewal fees commonly range from approximately AED 5,000-15,000+ depending on activity and office space requirements. Subject to change - confirm current fees on the relevant licensing authority's portal.
processing_time: Often same-day to a few business days once tenancy renewal and any outstanding dues are settled.

title: Emirates ID - Application and Renewal
eligibility: Mandatory for all UAE citizens and residents, including new residence visa holders (issued as part of the visa process) and existing residents whose Emirates ID is approaching expiry. Visitors and tourists are not required to obtain an Emirates ID.
documents: Passport copy, passport-size photo with white background, valid residence visa copy (for residents), proof of UAE address (in some cases), existing Emirates ID (for renewal).
steps: 1) Apply via ICP smart services, an approved typing center, or as part of the residence visa application process (for new visa holders, this is usually bundled in). 2) Complete biometric capture (fingerprints and photo) at an ICP-approved service center. 3) Pay applicable fees. 4) Receive the Emirates ID card by mail/delivery once processed, typically within a short period after biometrics are completed.
fees: Approximately AED 100-370 depending on the validity period chosen (commonly 1, 2, 3, 5, or 10 years, often tied to the residence visa duration), plus typing/service center fees and delivery charges if applicable. Subject to change - confirm current fees on the ICP portal.
processing_time: Typically a few days to about two weeks after biometrics are completed, though urgent/express services may be available at an additional fee in some cases. | Important: Emirates ID is required for nearly all government and many private-sector transactions in the UAE (banking, SIM cards, healthcare, etc.), so timely renewal before expiry is strongly recommended.
